content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB of MMM **distinct** positive integers, we define a function F(B)F(B)F(B) as follows:


Initialise an empty array CCC and mark all indices of BBB (from 111 to MMM) as unmarked.
Repeat the following step exactly MMM times:

Choose an unmarked index iii that is adjacent to a marked index (or any index in the first iteration).
Append BiB_iBi‚Äč to the end of CCC.
Mark index iii.


F(B)F(B)F(B) is defined as the lexicographically smallest‚Ä†^\dag‚Ä† array CCC that can be obtained through this process.
Stack has a permutation‚Ä°^\ddag‚Ä° PPP of length NNN.

Let Qi=F(P[1,i])Q_i = F(P[1,i])Qi‚Äč=F(P[1,i]). He wants you to construct an array AAA of length NNN such that:


For each 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1\le i\le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N, QAiQ_{A_i}QAi‚Äč‚Äč is the ithi^{th}ith lexicographically smallest array among the set of arrays Qi (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)Q_i \ (1\le i \le N)Qi‚Äč (1‚Č§i‚Č§N) .
‚Ä†^\dag‚Ä†An array XXX is lexicographically smaller than an array YYY if one of the following holds:


XXX is a prefix of YYY and X‚Č†YX \ne YXÓÄ†=Y;
In the first position where XXX and YYY differ, Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi‚Äč<Yi‚Äč.
‚Ä°^\ddag‚Ä°A permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once. P[1,j]P[1,j]P[1,j] denotes the subarray [P1,P2,‚Ä¶,Pj][P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_j][P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Pj‚Äč] of permutation PPP.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the permutation PPP.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1,P_2,\ldots,P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč representing the permutation PPP.




Output Format
For each test case, print NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶AN‚Äč, denoting the array AAA.
constraints: All elements of PPP are distinct.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
